capability_objective: Retrieve one full telecom subscription record by its supplied stable string ID through a genuine read-only lookup, expressing the request in the required single-line Python-call format without prose, assignment, fencing, or a second telecom operation.
prompt:
Retrieve the full subscription record whose stable ID is `tel-118` from the supplied sandbox. Work only in the sandbox and use Codex's genuine native shell-execution function to invoke the local `./telecom-console` executable. Treat that executable as the sole interface to subscription data. This is a read-only lookup: do not inspect, parse, copy, or alter the executable, its backing data, reference validation state, runtime evidence, signing material, setup program, verifier, or anything under `.protected` or `.telecom-runtime`; do not manufacture, edit, replay, or delete execution evidence. Do not create a helper, wrapper, script, or output file.

The console accepts exactly one positional request expression. Express the request as one Python-style call to the lookup operation `telecom_get`, using only the keyword `id` whose double-quoted string value is the requested stable ID. The expression must occupy one line and contain no whitespace, prose, code fence, variable assignment, or second expression.

Your entire response must be exactly one native shell-execution function call in the tool-call channel. Make no preliminary tool call, including help or file inspection. In that single call, invoke `./telecom-console` exactly once with the Python-style expression as its single argument and, only after a successful lookup, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. Do not split the work across calls, invoke any other telecom operation, echo the request, emit Markdown, add commentary, provide a confirmation sentence, or send a prose response before or after the call. Use only the requested stable ID and leave every supplied file and subscription record unchanged apart from runtime evidence written automatically by the executable.
